rawText:
That fiction can entertain is obvious; that it can instruct is widely conceded; but that it can constitute a genuine form of knowledge — irreducible to the propositional claims of philosophy or the empirical findings of science — remains a thesis that many thoughtful readers find difficult to accept.

The resistance stems from a deeply entrenched hierarchy of discourse in which narrative is subordinated to argument, metaphor to literal statement, and the particular to the general, as though the only truths worth having are those that can be stated in universal terms.

Yet the novelist who renders, with painstaking precision, the experience of a character navigating an unfamiliar city is doing something that no sociological survey can replicate: she is constructing a phenomenological account of what it feels like, from the inside, to be disoriented, alert, and vulnerable all at once.

This experiential dimension — what philosophers sometimes call "knowledge by acquaintance" as opposed to "knowledge by description" — is fiction's distinctive epistemic contribution, and it is not a lesser form of understanding simply because it resists the quantification that empirical disciplines demand.

Consider how differently we apprehend moral ambiguity after reading a novel in which a sympathetic character commits an act the reader simultaneously condemns and comprehends: the understanding that emerges is not a proposition to be memorized but a cognitive recalibration, a subtle restructuring of the categories through which we interpret human behavior.

Nor does this mean that fiction's knowledge is merely subjective; great novels, precisely because they immerse the reader in perspectives radically unlike her own, function as instruments of intersubjective understanding, expanding the boundaries of what one mind can apprehend about another.

The metaphor, often dismissed as ornamental, plays a central role in this epistemic process, for a well-constructed metaphor does not merely illustrate a pre-existing idea — it generates insight by forcing the reader to perceive a resemblance between domains she had previously considered unrelated.

When Toni Morrison writes that a character's loneliness "wore him thin as a dime," the reader does not simply understand that the character is lonely; she apprehends loneliness as material erosion, a metaphorical mapping that reconfigures her concept of suffering in ways no clinical definition could achieve.

It is this capacity for conceptual reconfiguration that distinguishes literary knowledge from other modes of understanding and justifies regarding fiction not as a decorative supplement to serious inquiry but as a serious inquiry in its own right.

The boundary between fiction and argument, moreover, is far more porous than the conventional taxonomy of genres suggests: essayists employ narrative to persuade, scientists use thought experiments indistinguishable from short stories, and philosophers from Plato onward have embedded their most challenging ideas within fictional dialogues.

To insist that knowledge must be propositional in form is to confuse one vehicle of understanding with understanding itself, overlooking the possibility that some truths can only be apprehended through the sustained imaginative engagement that narrative uniquely affords.

The question is not whether fiction "contains" knowledge as a textbook contains facts but whether the reader who has inhabited a richly imagined world emerges from it knowing something she did not know before — and the testimony of serious readers across centuries suggests the answer is unequivocally yes.

What fiction knows, it knows differently; and that difference, far from being a limitation, is precisely the source of its irreplaceable value.

If we are to cultivate a genuinely comprehensive epistemology — one adequate to the full range of human experience — then literature must be granted not merely a seat at the table but a voice in the conversation about what it means to understand.

overview.title: Fiction as a Way of Knowing

sentences.text: Stories can teach us things that facts and arguments cannot.
sentences.translation: 이야기는 사실과 논증이 가르칠 수 없는 것을 가르칠 수 있어요.

sentences.text: A novel helps us understand how someone else feels from the inside.
sentences.translation: 소설은 다른 사람이 안에서 어떻게 느끼는지 이해하는 데 도움을 줘요.

sentences.text: When we read about a character making a hard choice, our way of thinking changes a little.
sentences.translation: 등장인물이 어려운 선택을 하는 것을 읽으면, 우리의 사고방식이 조금 바뀌어요.

sentences.text: Good metaphors in stories help us see connections we never noticed before.
sentences.translation: 이야기 속 좋은 비유는 전에 알아차리지 못했던 연결고리를 볼 수 있게 해줘요.

sentences.text: The line between stories and arguments is not as clear as people think.
sentences.translation: 이야기와 논증 사이의 경계는 사람들이 생각하는 것만큼 명확하지 않아요.

sentences.text: Fiction gives us a kind of knowledge that is different but just as valuable.
sentences.translation: 소설은 다르지만 똑같이 가치 있는 종류의 지식을 줘요.

sentences.text: The essay argues that fiction constitutes a genuine form of knowledge, irreducible to the propositional claims of philosophy or science.
sentences.translation: 이 에세이는 소설이 철학이나 과학의 명제적 주장으로 환원할 수 없는 진정한 지식의 형태를 구성한다고 주장해요.

sentences.text: Fiction provides "knowledge by acquaintance" — understanding what something feels like from the inside — which empirical methods cannot replicate.
sentences.translation: 소설은 "체험에 의한 지식" — 내부에서 무언가가 어떤 느낌인지 이해하는 것 — 을 제공하며, 이는 경험적 방법으로 복제할 수 없어요.

sentences.text: Metaphors in literature generate genuine insight by revealing structural resemblances between previously unrelated domains.
sentences.translation: 문학의 비유는 이전에 관련 없다고 여겼던 영역 사이의 구조적 유사성을 드러내어 진정한 통찰을 생성해요.

sentences.text: The boundary between fiction and argument is far more porous than conventional genre distinctions suggest.
sentences.translation: 소설과 논증 사이의 경계는 전통적 장르 구분이 시사하는 것보다 훨씬 더 다공성이 있어요.

sentences.text: Insisting that knowledge must be propositional confuses one vehicle of understanding with understanding itself.
sentences.translation: 지식이 명제적이어야 한다고 고집하는 것은 이해의 한 수단을 이해 자체와 혼동하는 것이에요.

sentences.text: The essay concludes that a comprehensive epistemology must include literature as a legitimate way of knowing.
sentences.translation: 에세이는 포괄적 인식론이 정당한 앎의 방식으로서 문학을 포함해야 한다고 결론지어요.

sentences.text: This essay mounts an epistemological defense of fiction, drawing on the distinction between "knowledge by acquaintance" and "knowledge by description" to argue that narrative constitutes a distinctive cognitive mode.
sentences.translation: 이 에세이는 소설의 인식론적 방어를 전개하며, "체험에 의한 지식"과 "기술에 의한 지식"의 구분을 활용하여 서사가 독특한 인지 양식을 구성한다고 논증해요.

sentences.text: The writer challenges the entrenched hierarchy of discourse that privileges propositional over experiential knowledge, arguing this hierarchy rests on a category error.
sentences.translation: 글쓴이는 체험적 지식보다 명제적 지식을 우선시하는 뿌리 깊은 담론의 위계에 도전하며, 이 위계가 범주 오류에 기반한다고 주장해요.

sentences.text: Metaphor is repositioned from rhetorical ornament to epistemic instrument, following cognitive metaphor theory's claim that metaphor generates rather than merely illustrates understanding.
sentences.translation: 비유는 수사적 장식에서 인식론적 도구로 재배치되며, 비유가 이해를 단순히 예시하는 것이 아니라 생성한다는 인지적 비유 이론의 주장을 따르고 있어요.

sentences.text: The essay exploits the porosity of genre boundaries — citing essayists, scientists, and philosophers — to undermine the binary opposition between narrative and argument.
sentences.translation: 에세이는 에세이스트, 과학자, 철학자를 인용하며 장르 경계의 다공성을 활용하여, 서사와 논증 사이의 이항 대립을 허물어요.

sentences.text: The Morrison quotation functions as a performative demonstration: by causing the reader to experience metaphorical insight in real time, it enacts the very thesis it supports.
sentences.translation: 모리슨 인용문은 수행적 시연으로 기능해요: 독자가 실시간으로 비유적 통찰을 경험하게 함으로써, 그것이 지지하는 바로 그 논지를 실행해요.

sentences.text: The closing call for a "comprehensive epistemology" positions the essay within a broader philosophical project: the expansion of what counts as knowledge beyond the empirical-propositional paradigm.
sentences.translation: "포괄적 인식론"에 대한 마무리 호소는 에세이를 더 넓은 철학적 프로젝트 안에 위치시켜요: 경험적-명제적 패러다임을 넘어 지식으로 인정되는 것의 확장이에요.

sentences.original: That fiction can entertain is obvious; that it can instruct is widely conceded; but that it can constitute a genuine form of knowledge — irreducible to the propositional claims of philosophy or the empirical findings of science — remains a thesis that many thoughtful readers find difficult to accept.
sentences.translation: 소설이 즐거움을 줄 수 있다는 것은 자명하고; 가르침을 줄 수 있다는 것은 널리 인정되지만; 소설이 진정한 형태의 지식 — 철학의 명제적 주장이나 과학의 경험적 발견으로 환원할 수 없는 — 을 구성할 수 있다는 것은 많은 사려 깊은 독자들이 받아들이기 어려워하는 논제로 남아 있다.

grammarElements.text: That fiction can entertain
grammarElements.label: 주어 (that 명사절 1)

grammarElements.text: is obvious
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 보어

grammarElements.text: that it can instruct is widely conceded
grammarElements.label: 세미콜론 뒤 병렬절 (that 명사절 2)

grammarElements.text: but that it can constitute a genuine form of knowledge
grammarElements.label: 대조절 주어 (that 명사절 3)

grammarElements.text: irreducible to the propositional claims of philosophy or the empirical findings of science
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 형용사구

grammarElements.text: remains a thesis that many thoughtful readers find difficult to accept
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 보어 (관계절 포함)

vocabulary.example: Does fiction constitute genuine knowledge?
Propositional knowledge can be stated as true-or-false claims.
Literary knowledge is irreducible to simple statements.

expressions.phrase: That X is obvious; that Y is conceded; but that Z remains
expressions.meaning: X는 자명하고; Y는 인정되지만; Z는 ~로 남아 있다
expressions.usage: 세 개의 that절을 세미콜론과 but으로 연결하여 점진적 논쟁의 강도를 높이는 수사적 구조
어떤 것이 다른 것으로 완전히 설명될 수 없음을 나타내는 철학적 표현

patterns.pattern: That [X] is obvious; that [Y] is conceded; but that [Z] remains a thesis that [many find difficult to accept]
patterns.explanation: 세 개의 that 명사절을 점진적으로 배치하여 쉬운 주장에서 도전적 논제로 상승하는 삼단 구조. 에세이의 핵심 논쟁 지점을 도입한다.
examples: That technology can connect people is obvious; that it can educate them is widely conceded; but that it can replace the intimacy of face-to-face conversation remains a thesis that many sociologists find difficult to accept.

That data can inform decisions is obvious; that it can improve efficiency is widely conceded; but that it can capture the full complexity of human behavior remains a thesis that many psychologists find difficult to accept.

drillQuestions.question: "Propositional" knowledge is knowledge expressed as _____.
"Irreducible" means it _____ be simplified further.
The sentence uses _____ "that" clauses in parallel.
The progression moves from obvious to _____.

sentences.notes: 세 개의 that절이 점진적 상승 구조(자명 → 인정됨 → 논쟁적)를 형성하여 에세이의 핵심 논제를 도입한다. 대시 삽입구가 "knowledge"를 철학적으로 한정하고, "remains a thesis"가 이 논제의 논쟁적 지위를 표시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: That fiction can entertain is obvious
koreanAlignment.ko: 소설이 즐거움을 줄 수 있다는 것은 자명하고

koreanAlignment.en: that it can instruct is widely conceded
koreanAlignment.ko: 가르침을 줄 수 있다는 것은 널리 인정되지만

koreanAlignment.en: but that it can constitute a genuine form of knowledge
koreanAlignment.ko: 소설이 진정한 형태의 지식을 구성할 수 있다는 것은

koreanAlignment.en: irreducible to the propositional claims of philosophy or the empirical findings of science
koreanAlignment.ko: 철학의 명제적 주장이나 과학의 경험적 발견으로 환원할 수 없는

koreanAlignment.en: remains a thesis that many thoughtful readers find difficult to accept
koreanAlignment.ko: 많은 사려 깊은 독자들이 받아들이기 어려워하는 논제로 남아 있다

pronunciationNotes: propositional: /ˌprɑːpəˈzɪʃənəl/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "프라퍼지셔널"에 가깝다.
irreducible: /ˌɪrɪˈdjuːsəbəl/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "이리듀서블"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The resistance stems from a deeply entrenched hierarchy of discourse in which narrative is subordinated to argument, metaphor to literal statement, and the particular to the general, as though the only truths worth having are those that can be stated in universal terms.
sentences.translation: 그 저항은 서사가 논증에, 비유가 직설적 진술에, 특수가 보편에 종속되는 깊이 뿌리박힌 담론의 위계에서 비롯되며, 마치 가질 가치가 있는 유일한 진리는 보편적 용어로 진술할 수 있는 것뿐인 것처럼.

grammarElements.text: The resistance
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: stems from
grammarElements.label: 동사구

grammarElements.text: a deeply entrenched hierarchy of discourse
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: in which narrative is subordinated to argument, metaphor to literal statement, and the particular to the general
grammarElements.label: 관계절 (삼중 병렬 수동태)

grammarElements.text: as though the only truths worth having are those that can be stated in universal terms
grammarElements.label: as though 가정 부사절

vocabulary.example: The hierarchy of argument over narrative is deeply entrenched.
In this hierarchy, metaphor is subordinated to literal statement.

expressions.usage: 원인이나 기원을 밝힐 때 쓰는 격식적 동사구

expressions.phrase: as though the only truths worth having
expressions.meaning: 마치 가질 가치가 있는 유일한 진리가 ~인 것처럼
expressions.usage: "as though" 가정절로 비판 대상의 암묵적 전제를 노출하는 표현

patterns.pattern: The resistance stems from a deeply entrenched hierarchy in which [A is subordinated to B, C to D, and E to F], as though [the only X worth having are Y]
patterns.explanation: 저항의 원인을 위계 구조로 진단하고, 삼중 병렬로 종속 관계를 열거한 뒤, "as though"로 그 위계의 암묵적 전제를 비판적으로 노출하는 패턴.
examples: The skepticism stems from a deeply entrenched hierarchy in which quantitative data is subordinated to qualitative, observation to experiment, and description to explanation, as though the only evidence worth collecting is that which can be expressed numerically.

The dismissal stems from a deeply entrenched hierarchy in which practice is subordinated to theory, intuition to logic, and craft to science, as though the only knowledge worth having is that which can be formalized in equations.

drillQuestions.question: "Entrenched" means deeply _____.
"Subordinated to" means placed _____ in rank.
"As though" introduces a _____ assumption.
The hierarchy places argument _____ narrative.

sentences.notes: 삼중 병렬 종속(서사→논증, 비유→직설, 특수→보편)이 담론의 위계를 체계적으로 노출한다. "as though"절이 그 위계의 암묵적 전제를 비판적으로 드러내며, 에세이 전체가 이 위계에 도전하는 것임을 예고한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The resistance stems from
koreanAlignment.ko: 그 저항은 ~에서 비롯되며

koreanAlignment.en: a deeply entrenched hierarchy of discourse
koreanAlignment.ko: 깊이 뿌리박힌 담론의 위계

koreanAlignment.en: in which narrative is subordinated to argument, metaphor to literal statement, and the particular to the general
koreanAlignment.ko: 서사가 논증에, 비유가 직설적 진술에, 특수가 보편에 종속되는

koreanAlignment.en: as though the only truths worth having
koreanAlignment.ko: 마치 가질 가치가 있는 유일한 진리가

koreanAlignment.en: are those that can be stated in universal terms
koreanAlignment.ko: 보편적 용어로 진술할 수 있는 것뿐인 것처럼

pronunciationNotes: entrenched: /ɪnˈtrentʃt/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "인트렌치트"에 가깝다.
subordinated: /səˈbɔːrdɪneɪtɪd/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "서보디네이티드"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Yet the novelist who renders, with painstaking precision, the experience of a character navigating an unfamiliar city is doing something that no sociological survey can replicate: she is constructing a phenomenological account of what it feels like, from the inside, to be disoriented, alert, and vulnerable all at once.
sentences.translation: 그러나 낯선 도시를 항해하는 인물의 경험을 고통스러울 만큼의 정밀함으로 재현하는 소설가는 어떤 사회학적 조사도 복제할 수 없는 일을 하고 있다: 그녀는 내부에서 혼란스럽고, 경각하고, 취약한 상태가 동시에 어떤 느낌인지에 대한 현상학적 설명을 구축하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: Yet the novelist who renders, with painstaking precision, the experience of a character navigating an unfamiliar city
grammarElements.label: 주어 (관계절 + 삽입 부사구 + 분사구문)

grammarElements.text: is doing something that no sociological survey can replicate
grammarElements.label: 동사 (진행형 + 관계절)

grammarElements.text: she is constructing a phenomenological account
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 설명절

grammarElements.text: of what it feels like, from the inside, to be disoriented, alert, and vulnerable all at once
grammarElements.label: of + what절 (삽입구 + 삼중 형용사)

vocabulary.example: Fiction provides a phenomenological account of human experience.
The novelist renders detail with painstaking precision.
No survey can replicate the experiential dimension of fiction.

expressions.usage: 극도의 주의와 정확성을 강조하는 삽입 부사구

expressions.phrase: what it feels like, from the inside
expressions.usage: 주관적 경험의 내면성을 강조하는 표현

patterns.pattern: Yet the [person] who [renders/creates], with [quality], [experience] is doing something that no [method] can replicate: she is constructing [a phenomenological/experiential account of X]
patterns.explanation: "Yet"으로 앞 문장의 위계에 반론하고, 콜론 뒤에서 소설가의 작업이 경험적 방법으로 대체 불가능한 이유를 밝히는 패턴.
examples: Yet the poet who captures, with linguistic precision, the texture of grief is doing something that no psychological study can replicate: she is constructing an experiential account of what loss feels like from the inside.

Yet the filmmaker who portrays, with visual subtlety, the tension of a moral dilemma is doing something that no ethical treatise can replicate: he is constructing a phenomenological account of what it feels like to choose under pressure.

drillQuestions.question: "Phenomenological" relates to the study of _____.
"Painstaking" means extremely _____.
The colon introduces what the novelist is actually _____.
"All at once" means _____.

sentences.notes: "Yet"이 앞 문장의 위계에 대한 반론을 개시한다. 삽입구 "with painstaking precision"이 소설가의 기예를 강조하고, 콜론 뒤의 "phenomenological account"이 소설의 인식론적 기여를 철학적 용어로 명명한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Yet the novelist who renders, with painstaking precision
koreanAlignment.ko: 그러나 고통스러울 만큼의 정밀함으로 재현하는 소설가는

koreanAlignment.en: the experience of a character navigating an unfamiliar city
koreanAlignment.ko: 낯선 도시를 항해하는 인물의 경험을

koreanAlignment.en: is doing something that no sociological survey can replicate
koreanAlignment.ko: 어떤 사회학적 조사도 복제할 수 없는 일을 하고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: she is constructing a phenomenological account
koreanAlignment.ko: 그녀는 현상학적 설명을 구축하고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: of what it feels like, from the inside, to be disoriented, alert, and vulnerable all at once
koreanAlignment.ko: 내부에서 혼란스럽고, 경각하고, 취약한 상태가 동시에 어떤 느낌인지에 대한

pronunciationNotes: phenomenological: /fɪˌnɑːmɪnəˈlɑːdʒɪkəl/ — 다섯 번째 음절에 강세. "피나미널라지컬"에 가깝다.
painstaking: /ˈpeɪnzteɪkɪŋ/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "페인스테이킹"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: This experiential dimension — what philosophers sometimes call "knowledge by acquaintance" as opposed to "knowledge by description" — is fiction's distinctive epistemic contribution, and it is not a lesser form of understanding simply because it resists the quantification that empirical disciplines demand.
sentences.translation: 이 경험적 차원 — 철학자들이 때로 "기술에 의한 지식"에 대비하여 "체험에 의한 지식"이라고 부르는 것 — 은 소설의 고유한 인식론적 기여이며, 단순히 경험 학문이 요구하는 정량화에 저항한다는 이유로 더 낮은 형태의 이해가 되는 것은 아니다.

grammarElements.text: This experiential dimension
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: what philosophers sometimes call "knowledge by acquaintance" as opposed to "knowledge by description"
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 동격절 (what절 + as opposed to)

grammarElements.text: is fiction's distinctive epistemic contribution
grammarElements.label: 보어

grammarElements.text: and it is not a lesser form of understanding
grammarElements.label: 병렬절 (부정)

grammarElements.text: simply because it resists the quantification that empirical disciplines demand
grammarElements.label: because 이유절 (관계절 포함)

vocabulary.example: Fiction makes a distinctive epistemic contribution.
Knowledge by acquaintance comes from direct experience.
Some forms of knowledge resist quantification.

expressions.usage: 두 개념을 명시적으로 대비하는 학술적 표현

expressions.phrase: not a lesser form simply because
expressions.meaning: 단순히 ~이라는 이유로 더 낮은 형태가 되는 것은 아니다
expressions.usage: 특정 이유에 의한 격하를 부정하는 논증적 표현

patterns.pattern: This [dimension] — what [experts] call "[term A]" as opposed to "[term B]" — is [X's] distinctive [contribution], and it is not a lesser form simply because it [resists what Y demands]
patterns.explanation: 대시 삽입구로 전문 용어를 도입하고, "not a lesser form simply because"로 격하를 부정하는 방어적 논증 패턴.
examples: This intuitive dimension — what psychologists call "implicit knowledge" as opposed to "explicit knowledge" — is expertise's distinctive contribution, and it is not a lesser form of competence simply because it resists the articulation that academic discourse demands.

This tacit dimension — what practitioners call "craft knowledge" as opposed to "theoretical knowledge" — is apprenticeship's distinctive contribution, and it is not a lesser form of skill simply because it resists the codification that textbooks demand.

drillQuestions.question: "Epistemic" relates to _____.
"Knowledge by acquaintance" is gained through _____ experience.
"Resists quantification" means it is hard to _____.
"Not a lesser form simply because" defends against _____.

sentences.notes: 철학적 구분("knowledge by acquaintance" vs. "knowledge by description")을 대시 삽입구로 도입하여 소설의 인식론적 지위를 공식화한다. "not a lesser form simply because"가 정량화 불가능성에 의한 격하를 선제적으로 반박한다.

koreanAlignment.en: This experiential dimension
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 경험적 차원은

koreanAlignment.en: what philosophers sometimes call "knowledge by acquaintance" as opposed to "knowledge by description"
koreanAlignment.ko: 철학자들이 "기술에 의한 지식"에 대비하여 "체험에 의한 지식"이라고 부르는 것

koreanAlignment.en: is fiction's distinctive epistemic contribution
koreanAlignment.ko: 소설의 고유한 인식론적 기여이며

koreanAlignment.en: and it is not a lesser form of understanding
koreanAlignment.ko: 더 낮은 형태의 이해가 되는 것은 아니다

koreanAlignment.en: simply because it resists the quantification that empirical disciplines demand
koreanAlignment.ko: 단순히 경험 학문이 요구하는 정량화에 저항한다는 이유로

pronunciationNotes: epistemic: /ˌepɪˈstemɪk/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "에피스테믹"에 가깝다.
acquaintance: /əˈkweɪntəns/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "어퀘인턴스"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Consider how differently we apprehend moral ambiguity after reading a novel in which a sympathetic character commits an act the reader simultaneously condemns and comprehends: the understanding that emerges is not a proposition to be memorized but a cognitive recalibration, a subtle restructuring of the categories through which we interpret human behavior.
sentences.translation: 공감할 수 있는 인물이 독자가 동시에 비난하면서도 이해하는 행위를 저지르는 소설을 읽은 후 우리가 도덕적 모호함을 얼마나 다르게 파악하게 되는지 생각해 보라: 출현하는 이해는 암기할 명제가 아니라 인지적 재조정, 우리가 인간 행동을 해석하는 범주의 미묘한 재구조화이다.

grammarElements.text: Consider how differently we apprehend moral ambiguity
grammarElements.label: 명령문 + how 간접의문문

grammarElements.text: after reading a novel in which a sympathetic character commits an act the reader simultaneously condemns and comprehends
grammarElements.label: after 전치사구 (관계절 중첩)

grammarElements.text: the understanding that emerges is not a proposition to be memorized but a cognitive recalibration
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 설명절 (not A but B)

grammarElements.text: a subtle restructuring of the categories through which we interpret human behavior
grammarElements.label: 동격 명사구 (관계절 포함)

vocabulary.example: We apprehend moral complexity differently after reading great fiction.
Reading fiction causes a cognitive recalibration.

expressions.usage: 도덕적 모호함에 대한 이중적 반응을 표현하는 문학적 대비

expressions.phrase: a subtle restructuring of the categories
expressions.usage: 인지적 변화를 학술적으로 기술하는 표현

patterns.pattern: Consider how differently we [apprehend X] after [experiencing Y]: the understanding that emerges is not [a proposition] but [a recalibration], a subtle [restructuring of Z]
patterns.explanation: "Consider" 명령문으로 독자를 자기 경험으로 초대하고, 콜론 뒤의 "not A but B"로 출현하는 이해의 성격을 재정의하는 패턴.
examples: Consider how differently we apprehend forgiveness after watching a film in which the protagonist pardons an undeserving antagonist: the understanding that emerges is not a moral rule to be followed but a cognitive recalibration, a subtle expansion of what we thought possible.

Consider how differently we apprehend loneliness after reading a memoir of isolation: the understanding that emerges is not a clinical diagnosis but a perceptual recalibration, a subtle deepening of our empathetic range.

drillQuestions.question: "Apprehend" in this context means to _____.
"Recalibration" means a process of _____.
The reader "simultaneously condemns and comprehends," showing _____.
The understanding is described as a restructuring of _____.

sentences.notes: "simultaneously condemns and comprehends"가 도덕적 모호함에 대한 소설적 경험의 핵심을 포착한다. "not a proposition but a cognitive recalibration"이 소설적 지식의 비명제적 성격을 정확히 기술하며, 에세이의 중심 주장을 뒷받침한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Consider how differently we apprehend moral ambiguity
koreanAlignment.ko: 우리가 도덕적 모호함을 얼마나 다르게 파악하게 되는지 생각해 보라

koreanAlignment.en: after reading a novel in which a sympathetic character commits an act
koreanAlignment.ko: 공감할 수 있는 인물이 행위를 저지르는 소설을 읽은 후

koreanAlignment.en: the reader simultaneously condemns and comprehends
koreanAlignment.ko: 독자가 동시에 비난하면서도 이해하는

koreanAlignment.en: the understanding that emerges is not a proposition to be memorized
koreanAlignment.ko: 출현하는 이해는 암기할 명제가 아니라

koreanAlignment.en: but a cognitive recalibration, a subtle restructuring of the categories through which we interpret human behavior
koreanAlignment.ko: 인지적 재조정, 우리가 인간 행동을 해석하는 범주의 미묘한 재구조화이다

pronunciationNotes: apprehend: /ˌæprɪˈhend/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "애프리헨드"에 가깝다.
recalibration: /ˌriːkælɪˈbreɪʃən/ — 네 번째 음절에 강세. "리캘리브레이션"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Nor does this mean that fiction's knowledge is merely subjective; great novels, precisely because they immerse the reader in perspectives radically unlike her own, function as instruments of intersubjective understanding, expanding the boundaries of what one mind can apprehend about another.
sentences.translation: 이것이 소설의 지식이 단지 주관적이라는 의미도 아니다; 위대한 소설은 독자를 자신의 것과 근본적으로 다른 관점에 몰입시키기 때문에 정확히, 간주관적 이해의 도구로 기능하며, 한 마음이 다른 마음에 대해 파악할 수 있는 것의 경계를 확장한다.

grammarElements.text: Nor does this mean that fiction's knowledge is merely subjective
grammarElements.label: 도치 부정문 (Nor + 도치)

grammarElements.text: great novels
grammarElements.label: 세미콜론 뒤 주어

grammarElements.text: precisely because they immerse the reader in perspectives radically unlike her own
grammarElements.label: 이유 부사절 (precisely because)

grammarElements.text: function as instruments of intersubjective understanding
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 보어

grammarElements.text: expanding the boundaries of what one mind can apprehend about another
grammarElements.label: 분사구문

vocabulary.example: Fiction enables intersubjective understanding across radically different perspectives.
Great novels immerse the reader in unfamiliar perspectives.

expressions.usage: "Nor + 도치"로 가능한 오해를 선제적으로 차단하는 학술적 표현
인과관계를 강조하여 정확한 이유를 밝히는 표현

patterns.pattern: Nor does this mean that [X is merely Y]; [Z], precisely because [they do A], function as instruments of [B], expanding [C]
patterns.explanation: "Nor + 도치"로 오해를 차단하고, "precisely because"로 정확한 이유를 밝힌 뒤, 분사구문으로 결과를 확장하는 패턴.
examples: Nor does this mean that personal experience is merely anecdotal; first-person accounts, precisely because they capture nuances that statistics miss, function as instruments of qualitative understanding, expanding our sense of what data alone cannot reveal.

Nor does this mean that emotional responses are merely irrational; empathetic reactions, precisely because they bypass purely logical processing, function as instruments of moral intuition, expanding our capacity to perceive injustice.

drillQuestions.question: "Intersubjective" means shared between _____.
"Nor does this mean" prevents a possible _____.
Novels "immerse" the reader, meaning they _____ them deeply.
"Expanding the boundaries" suggests fiction _____ understanding.

sentences.notes: "Nor + 도치"가 주관성 비판을 선제적으로 차단한다. "intersubjective understanding"이 소설적 지식의 사회적 차원을 밝히며, 분사구문 "expanding the boundaries"가 소설의 인식론적 확장 기능을 표현한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Nor does this mean that fiction's knowledge is merely subjective
koreanAlignment.ko: 이것이 소설의 지식이 단지 주관적이라는 의미도 아니다

koreanAlignment.en: great novels, precisely because they immerse the reader in perspectives radically unlike her own
koreanAlignment.ko: 위대한 소설은 독자를 자신의 것과 근본적으로 다른 관점에 몰입시키기 때문에 정확히

koreanAlignment.en: function as instruments of intersubjective understanding
koreanAlignment.ko: 간주관적 이해의 도구로 기능하며

koreanAlignment.en: expanding the boundaries of what one mind can apprehend about another
koreanAlignment.ko: 한 마음이 다른 마음에 대해 파악할 수 있는 것의 경계를 확장한다

pronunciationNotes: intersubjective: /ˌɪntərsəbˈdʒektɪv/ — 네 번째 음절에 강세. "인터서브젝티브"에 가깝다.
immerse: /ɪˈmɜːrs/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "이머스"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The metaphor, often dismissed as ornamental, plays a central role in this epistemic process, for a well-constructed metaphor does not merely illustrate a pre-existing idea — it generates insight by forcing the reader to perceive a resemblance between domains she had previously considered unrelated.
sentences.translation: 종종 장식적인 것으로 일축되는 비유는 이 인식론적 과정에서 중심적 역할을 하는데, 잘 구성된 비유는 기존의 아이디어를 단순히 예시하는 것이 아니라 — 독자가 이전에 무관하다고 여겼던 영역 사이의 유사성을 인지하도록 강제함으로써 통찰을 생성하기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: The metaphor, often dismissed as ornamental,
grammarElements.label: 주어 (삽입 분사구문)

grammarElements.text: plays a central role in this epistemic process
grammarElements.label: 동사구

grammarElements.text: for a well-constructed metaphor does not merely illustrate a pre-existing idea
grammarElements.label: for 이유절 (부정)

grammarElements.text: it generates insight by forcing the reader to perceive a resemblance between domains she had previously considered unrelated
grammarElements.label: 대시 뒤 대조절

vocabulary.example: Metaphor is more than ornamental language.
Metaphor reveals a resemblance between unlike domains.

expressions.usage: 일반적 평가절하를 지적하며 재평가를 예고하는 삽입 분사구문

expressions.phrase: does not merely illustrate — it generates
expressions.usage: 부정 뒤 대시로 더 강한 주장을 도입하는 대조적 수사

patterns.pattern: The [X], often dismissed as [Y], plays a central role in [Z], for [X] does not merely [illustrate] — it [generates] by [forcing the reader to perceive]
patterns.explanation: 평가절하된 대상을 재평가하는 패턴. 삽입 분사구문으로 기존 평가를 지적하고, "does not merely A — it B"로 재정의한다.
examples: The anecdote, often dismissed as unscientific, plays a central role in persuasion, for a well-chosen anecdote does not merely entertain — it generates conviction by forcing the listener to inhabit a perspective she had not previously considered.

The question, often dismissed as pedagogically basic, plays a central role in deep learning, for a well-timed question does not merely test comprehension — it generates understanding by forcing the student to articulate connections she had not previously recognized.

drillQuestions.question: "Ornamental" means serving as _____.
Metaphor "generates" insight, meaning it _____ it.
"Domains she had previously considered unrelated" means _____ areas.
The dash signals a shift from weak to _____ claim.

sentences.notes: 비유의 재평가 — 장식에서 인식론적 도구로 — 가 이 문장의 핵심이다. "does not merely illustrate — it generates"의 대조가 비유의 인지적 능동성을 강조하며, 인지적 비유 이론(cognitive metaphor theory)의 핵심 주장을 반영한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The metaphor, often dismissed as ornamental
koreanAlignment.ko: 종종 장식적인 것으로 일축되는 비유는

koreanAlignment.en: plays a central role in this epistemic process
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 인식론적 과정에서 중심적 역할을 하는데

koreanAlignment.en: for a well-constructed metaphor does not merely illustrate a pre-existing idea
koreanAlignment.ko: 잘 구성된 비유는 기존의 아이디어를 단순히 예시하는 것이 아니라

koreanAlignment.en: it generates insight by forcing the reader to perceive a resemblance
koreanAlignment.ko: 유사성을 인지하도록 강제함으로써 통찰을 생성하기 때문이다

koreanAlignment.en: between domains she had previously considered unrelated
koreanAlignment.ko: 이전에 무관하다고 여겼던 영역 사이의

pronunciationNotes: ornamental: /ˌɔːrnəˈmentəl/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "오너멘틀"에 가깝다.
resemblance: /rɪˈzembləns/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "리젬블런스"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: When Toni Morrison writes that a character's loneliness "wore him thin as a dime," the reader does not simply understand that the character is lonely; she apprehends loneliness as material erosion, a metaphorical mapping that reconfigures her concept of suffering in ways no clinical definition could achieve.
sentences.translation: 토니 모리슨이 등장인물의 외로움이 "그를 동전처럼 얇게 닳게 했다"고 쓸 때, 독자는 단순히 그 인물이 외롭다는 것을 이해하는 것이 아니다; 그녀는 외로움을 물질적 침식으로 파악하며, 이는 어떤 임상적 정의도 달성할 수 없는 방식으로 고통에 대한 개념을 재구성하는 비유적 사상이다.

grammarElements.text: When Toni Morrison writes that a character's loneliness "wore him thin as a dime"
grammarElements.label: 시간 부사절 (that 명사절 + 직접 인용)

grammarElements.text: the reader does not simply understand that the character is lonely
grammarElements.label: 주절 (부정)

grammarElements.text: she apprehends loneliness as material erosion
grammarElements.label: 세미콜론 뒤 재해석절

grammarElements.text: a metaphorical mapping that reconfigures her concept of suffering in ways no clinical definition could achieve
grammarElements.label: 동격 명사구 (관계절 포함)

vocabulary.example: The metaphorical mapping between loneliness and erosion creates new understanding.
Great metaphors reconfigure our conceptual frameworks.

expressions.phrase: wore him thin as a dime
expressions.usage: 모리슨의 문학적 비유: 외로움을 물질적 마모로 표현한 은유

expressions.phrase: in ways no clinical definition could achieve
expressions.meaning: 어떤 임상적 정의도 달성할 수 없는 방식으로
expressions.usage: 문학적 비유의 우월성을 주장하는 비교 표현

patterns.pattern: When [author] writes that "[metaphor]," the reader does not simply understand [X]; she apprehends [X as Y], a metaphorical mapping that reconfigures [concept] in ways no [definition] could achieve
patterns.explanation: 구체적 문학 인용을 제시한 뒤, 독자의 인지적 경험을 분석하여 비유의 인식론적 기능을 수행적으로 시연하는 패턴.
examples: When Kafka writes that Gregor Samsa "woke up to find himself transformed into a monstrous insect," the reader does not simply understand alienation; she apprehends identity as something fragile and contingent, a metaphorical mapping that reconfigures her concept of selfhood in ways no philosophical treatise could achieve.

When Dickinson writes that "hope is the thing with feathers," the reader does not simply understand optimism; she apprehends hope as living and autonomous, a metaphorical mapping that reconfigures her concept of resilience in ways no psychological manual could achieve.

drillQuestions.question: "Wore him thin as a dime" compares loneliness to physical _____.
"Metaphorical mapping" transfers meaning between two _____.
"Reconfigures" means to _____ the structure of something.
The comparison to "clinical definition" argues for literature's _____.

sentences.notes: 모리슨 인용문이 에세이의 논지를 수행적으로 시연한다: 독자는 이 문장을 읽으면서 실제로 비유적 통찰을 경험한다. "metaphorical mapping"이 인지적 비유 이론의 핵심 개념을 도입하고, "no clinical definition could achieve"가 문학적 비유의 인식론적 우위를 주장한다.

koreanAlignment.en: When Toni Morrison writes that a character's loneliness "wore him thin as a dime"
koreanAlignment.ko: 토니 모리슨이 등장인물의 외로움이 "그를 동전처럼 얇게 닳게 했다"고 쓸 때

koreanAlignment.en: the reader does not simply understand that the character is lonely
koreanAlignment.ko: 독자는 단순히 그 인물이 외롭다는 것을 이해하는 것이 아니다

koreanAlignment.en: she apprehends loneliness as material erosion
koreanAlignment.ko: 그녀는 외로움을 물질적 침식으로 파악하며

koreanAlignment.en: a metaphorical mapping that reconfigures her concept of suffering
koreanAlignment.ko: 고통에 대한 개념을 재구성하는 비유적 사상이다

koreanAlignment.en: in ways no clinical definition could achieve
koreanAlignment.ko: 어떤 임상적 정의도 달성할 수 없는 방식으로

pronunciationNotes: metaphorical: /ˌmetəˈfɔːrɪkəl/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "메터포리컬"에 가깝다.
reconfigures: /ˌriːkənˈfɪɡjərz/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "리컨피겨즈"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: It is this capacity for conceptual reconfiguration that distinguishes literary knowledge from other modes of understanding and justifies regarding fiction not as a decorative supplement to serious inquiry but as a serious inquiry in its own right.
sentences.translation: 다른 이해 양식과 문학적 지식을 구별하고 소설을 진지한 탐구의 장식적 보충이 아닌 그 자체로 진지한 탐구로 간주하는 것을 정당화하는 것은 바로 이 개념적 재구성의 능력이다.

grammarElements.text: It is this capacity for conceptual reconfiguration
grammarElements.label: 강조 구문 (It is ... that)

grammarElements.text: that distinguishes literary knowledge from other modes of understanding
grammarElements.label: 강조 관계절 1

grammarElements.text: and justifies regarding fiction not as a decorative supplement to serious inquiry but as a serious inquiry in its own right
grammarElements.label: 강조 관계절 2 (not as A but as B)

vocabulary.example: Fiction's power lies in conceptual reconfiguration.
Fiction is not a supplement but a form of inquiry.

expressions.usage: 분열문(cleft sentence)으로 핵심 논점을 강조하는 구조
독립적 가치를 강조하는 표현

patterns.pattern: It is this [capacity/quality] that distinguishes [X] from [Y] and justifies regarding [X] not as [lesser thing] but as [equal thing] in its own right
patterns.explanation: 분열문으로 핵심 능력을 강조하고, "not as A but as B in its own right"로 대상의 독립적 지위를 선언하는 패턴.
examples: It is this capacity for emotional precision that distinguishes poetry from prose and justifies regarding verse not as a decorative form of expression but as a cognitive instrument in its own right.

It is this capacity for spatial reasoning that distinguishes architecture from mere construction and justifies regarding design not as an aesthetic luxury but as an intellectual discipline in its own right.

drillQuestions.question: "It is this capacity that" is a _____ sentence structure.
"In its own right" means as something _____.
Fiction is repositioned from "supplement" to _____.
"Conceptual reconfiguration" is fiction's key _____ power.

sentences.notes: 분열문(It is ... that) 구조가 "conceptual reconfiguration"을 에세이의 핵심 논점으로 격상시킨다. "not as a decorative supplement but as a serious inquiry in its own right"가 소설의 인식론적 독립성을 최종 선언한다.

koreanAlignment.en: It is this capacity for conceptual reconfiguration
koreanAlignment.ko: 바로 이 개념적 재구성의 능력이

koreanAlignment.en: that distinguishes literary knowledge from other modes of understanding
koreanAlignment.ko: 문학적 지식을 다른 이해 양식과 구별하고

koreanAlignment.en: and justifies regarding fiction not as a decorative supplement to serious inquiry
koreanAlignment.ko: 소설을 진지한 탐구의 장식적 보충이 아닌

koreanAlignment.en: but as a serious inquiry in its own right
koreanAlignment.ko: 그 자체로 진지한 탐구로 간주하는 것을 정당화하는 것이다

pronunciationNotes: reconfiguration: /ˌriːkənˌfɪɡjəˈreɪʃən/ — 다섯 번째 음절에 강세. "리컨피겨레이션"에 가깝다.
supplement: /ˈsʌplɪmənt/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "서플리먼트"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The boundary between fiction and argument, moreover, is far more porous than the conventional taxonomy of genres suggests: essayists employ narrative to persuade, scientists use thought experiments indistinguishable from short stories, and philosophers from Plato onward have embedded their most challenging ideas within fictional dialogues.
sentences.translation: 게다가, 소설과 논증 사이의 경계는 전통적 장르 분류법이 시사하는 것보다 훨씬 더 다공성이 높다: 에세이스트는 서사를 사용하여 설득하고, 과학자는 단편소설과 구별 불가능한 사고 실험을 사용하며, 플라톤 이래의 철학자들은 가장 도전적인 아이디어를 소설적 대화 안에 내장해 왔다.

grammarElements.text: The boundary between fiction and argument, moreover,
grammarElements.label: 주어 (삽입 접속부사)

grammarElements.text: is far more porous than the conventional taxonomy of genres suggests
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 비교급 구조

grammarElements.text: essayists employ narrative to persuade
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 예시 1

grammarElements.text: scientists use thought experiments indistinguishable from short stories
grammarElements.label: 예시 2

grammarElements.text: and philosophers from Plato onward have embedded their most challenging ideas within fictional dialogues
grammarElements.label: 예시 3

vocabulary.example: Genre boundaries are more porous than we assume.
The conventional taxonomy of genres separates fiction from argument.

expressions.usage: 경계의 허술함을 강조하는 비유적 비교 표현
역사적 기원과 연속성을 나타내는 시간 표현

patterns.pattern: The boundary between [A] and [B], moreover, is far more porous than [conventional wisdom] suggests: [example 1], [example 2], and [example 3]
patterns.explanation: "moreover"로 부가적 논점을 도입하고, 콜론 뒤 삼중 예시로 경계의 다공성을 구체적으로 증명하는 패턴.
examples: The boundary between art and commerce, moreover, is far more porous than purists suggest: painters accept commissions, musicians compose for advertisements, and writers from Shakespeare onward have crafted their greatest works with financial incentives in mind.

The boundary between teaching and research, moreover, is far more porous than institutional structures suggest: lecturers discover insights while explaining, students generate hypotheses while learning, and scholars from Socrates onward have used dialogue as a method of inquiry.

drillQuestions.question: "Porous" means having many _____ that allow passage.
"Taxonomy" means a system of _____.
The three examples show that fiction and argument _____.
"From Plato onward" emphasizes _____ tradition.

sentences.notes: "porous"가 경계의 물질적 비유를 제공하고, 삼중 예시(에세이스트, 과학자, 철학자)가 소설-논증 구분의 허구성을 구체적으로 증명한다. "from Plato onward"가 이 다공성의 역사적 깊이를 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: The boundary between fiction and argument, moreover, is far more porous
koreanAlignment.ko: 게다가 소설과 논증 사이의 경계는 훨씬 더 다공성이 높다

koreanAlignment.en: than the conventional taxonomy of genres suggests
koreanAlignment.ko: 전통적 장르 분류법이 시사하는 것보다

koreanAlignment.en: essayists employ narrative to persuade
koreanAlignment.ko: 에세이스트는 서사를 사용하여 설득하고

koreanAlignment.en: scientists use thought experiments indistinguishable from short stories
koreanAlignment.ko: 과학자는 단편소설과 구별 불가능한 사고 실험을 사용하며

koreanAlignment.en: and philosophers from Plato onward have embedded their most challenging ideas within fictional dialogues
koreanAlignment.ko: 플라톤 이래의 철학자들은 가장 도전적인 아이디어를 소설적 대화 안에 내장해 왔다

pronunciationNotes: porous: /ˈpɔːrəs/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "포러스"에 가깝다.
taxonomy: /tækˈsɑːnəmi/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "택사너미"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: To insist that knowledge must be propositional in form is to confuse one vehicle of understanding with understanding itself, overlooking the possibility that some truths can only be apprehended through the sustained imaginative engagement that narrative uniquely affords.
sentences.translation: 지식이 형식에서 명제적이어야 한다고 고집하는 것은 이해의 한 수단을 이해 자체와 혼동하는 것이며, 일부 진리는 서사가 유일하게 제공하는 지속적인 상상적 참여를 통해서만 파악될 수 있다는 가능성을 간과하는 것이다.

grammarElements.text: To insist that knowledge must be propositional in form
grammarElements.label: 주어 (to 부정사구 + that 명사절)

grammarElements.text: is to confuse one vehicle of understanding with understanding itself
grammarElements.label: 보어 (to 부정사구)

grammarElements.text: overlooking the possibility that some truths can only be apprehended through the sustained imaginative engagement that narrative uniquely affords
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (that 명사절 + 관계절)

vocabulary.example: Propositional language is only one vehicle of understanding.
Narrative affords a kind of engagement that argument cannot.

expressions.phrase: To insist X is to confuse Y with Z
expressions.meaning: X를 고집하는 것은 Y를 Z와 혼동하는 것이다
expressions.usage: "To A is to B" 구조로 논리적 오류를 지적하는 격식적 논증 표현
서사 읽기의 인지적 특성을 기술하는 학술적 표현

patterns.pattern: To insist that [X must be Y] is to confuse [one vehicle] with [the thing itself], overlooking the possibility that [some truths require Z]
patterns.explanation: "To A is to B" 구조로 논리적 오류를 진단하고, 분사구문으로 간과된 가능성을 제시하는 패턴.
examples: To insist that intelligence must be measurable is to confuse one indicator of ability with ability itself, overlooking the possibility that some forms of brilliance can only be recognized through extended observation.

To insist that communication must be verbal is to confuse one channel of expression with expression itself, overlooking the possibility that some meanings can only be conveyed through gesture, silence, or art.

drillQuestions.question: "To insist X is to confuse" points out a logical _____.
"Vehicle of understanding" means a _____ of understanding.
"Uniquely affords" means only _____ provides.
"Overlooking" means _____ to notice.

sentences.notes: "To A is to B" 구조가 명제적 지식 독점을 논리적 오류(범주 혼동)로 진단한다. "overlooking the possibility" 분사구문이 간과된 대안을 제시하며, "sustained imaginative engagement"가 소설적 앎의 조건을 정의한다.

koreanAlignment.en: To insist that knowledge must be propositional in form
koreanAlignment.ko: 지식이 형식에서 명제적이어야 한다고 고집하는 것은

koreanAlignment.en: is to confuse one vehicle of understanding with understanding itself
koreanAlignment.ko: 이해의 한 수단을 이해 자체와 혼동하는 것이며

koreanAlignment.en: overlooking the possibility that some truths can only be apprehended
koreanAlignment.ko: 일부 진리는 ~를 통해서만 파악될 수 있다는 가능성을 간과하는 것이다

koreanAlignment.en: through the sustained imaginative engagement that narrative uniquely affords
koreanAlignment.ko: 서사가 유일하게 제공하는 지속적인 상상적 참여를 통해

pronunciationNotes: vehicle: /ˈviːɪkəl/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "비이클"에 가깝다.
affords: /əˈfɔːrdz/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "어포즈"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The question is not whether fiction "contains" knowledge as a textbook contains facts but whether the reader who has inhabited a richly imagined world emerges from it knowing something she did not know before — and the testimony of serious readers across centuries suggests the answer is unequivocally yes.
sentences.translation: 질문은 소설이 교과서가 사실을 담듯 지식을 "담고 있는지" 여부가 아니라, 풍부하게 상상된 세계에 거주한 독자가 이전에 몰랐던 무언가를 알게 된 채로 그것에서 나오는지 여부이며 — 수세기에 걸친 진지한 독자들의 증언은 그 답이 분명히 긍정적임을 시사한다.

grammarElements.text: The question is not whether fiction "contains" knowledge as a textbook contains facts
grammarElements.label: 주절 (not whether A as B)

grammarElements.text: but whether the reader who has inhabited a richly imagined world emerges from it knowing something she did not know before
grammarElements.label: but whether절 (관계절 + 분사구문)

grammarElements.text: and the testimony of serious readers across centuries suggests the answer is unequivocally yes
grammarElements.label: 대시 뒤 병렬절

vocabulary.example: The reader who has inhabited a fictional world gains experiential knowledge.
The answer, across centuries, is unequivocally yes.

expressions.meaning: 무언가를 알게 된 채로 그것에서 나오다
expressions.usage: 독서 경험의 인식론적 결과를 표현하는 문학적 표현

expressions.phrase: the testimony of serious readers across centuries
expressions.usage: 역사적 증거를 소환하여 논지를 뒷받침하는 학술적 표현

patterns.pattern: The question is not whether [X "contains" Y] but whether [the person who has experienced X] emerges [knowing something new] — and [the evidence] suggests the answer is unequivocally [yes/no]
patterns.explanation: "not whether A but whether B"로 질문을 재구성하고, 대시 뒤에 역사적 증거로 확정적 답을 제시하는 마무리 패턴.
examples: The question is not whether travel "contains" education as a curriculum contains lessons but whether the traveler who has navigated unfamiliar cultures emerges understanding something she did not understand before — and the testimony of thoughtful travelers across centuries suggests the answer is unequivocally yes.

The question is not whether conversation "contains" therapy as a manual contains techniques but whether the person who has engaged in honest dialogue emerges feeling something she did not feel before — and the experience of countless participants suggests the answer is unequivocally yes.

drillQuestions.question: "Inhabited" a fictional world means having _____ in it mentally.
"Unequivocally" means without any _____.
The question shifts from "containing" to _____ from fiction.
The "testimony across centuries" provides _____ evidence.

sentences.notes: "contains"에 인용 부호를 사용하여 컨테이너 비유의 부적절함을 표시한다. "inhabited"가 독서를 거주로 비유하며, "unequivocally yes"가 에세이의 확신적 답을 제시한다. 대시가 질문에서 답으로의 전환을 표시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The question is not whether fiction "contains" knowledge as a textbook contains facts
koreanAlignment.ko: 질문은 소설이 교과서가 사실을 담듯 지식을 "담고 있는지" 여부가 아니라

koreanAlignment.en: but whether the reader who has inhabited a richly imagined world
koreanAlignment.ko: 풍부하게 상상된 세계에 거주한 독자가

koreanAlignment.en: emerges from it knowing something she did not know before
koreanAlignment.ko: 이전에 몰랐던 무언가를 알게 된 채로 그것에서 나오는지 여부이며

koreanAlignment.en: and the testimony of serious readers across centuries suggests the answer is unequivocally yes
koreanAlignment.ko: 수세기에 걸친 진지한 독자들의 증언은 그 답이 분명히 긍정적임을 시사한다

pronunciationNotes: inhabited: /ɪnˈhæbɪtɪd/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "인해비티드"에 가깝다.
unequivocally: /ˌʌnɪˈkwɪvəkəli/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "어니퀴버컬리"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: What fiction knows, it knows differently; and that difference, far from being a limitation, is precisely the source of its irreplaceable value.
sentences.translation: 소설이 아는 것을, 소설은 다르게 안다; 그리고 그 차이는 한계이기는커녕, 정확히 그것의 대체 불가능한 가치의 원천이다.

grammarElements.text: What fiction knows
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (관계대명사절, 전치)

grammarElements.text: it knows differently
grammarElements.label: 주절 (부사 수식)

grammarElements.text: and that difference
grammarElements.label: 세미콜론 뒤 주어

grammarElements.text: far from being a limitation
grammarElements.label: 삽입 분사구문 (양보)

grammarElements.text: is precisely the source of its irreplaceable value
grammarElements.label: 보어

vocabulary.example: Fiction's value is irreplaceable because no other mode of knowing can replicate it.

expressions.phrase: What X knows, it knows differently
expressions.meaning: X가 아는 것을, X는 다르게 안다
expressions.usage: 목적어 전치 + 대명사 반복으로 강조하는 문학적 병렬 표현
"far from + 동명사"로 예상되는 부정적 평가를 강하게 부정하는 표현

patterns.pattern: What [X] knows, it knows differently; and that difference, far from being [a limitation], is precisely the source of [its value]
patterns.explanation: 목적어 전치와 세미콜론 병렬로 핵심 역설을 응축한 격언적 문장. "far from being"이 부정적 평가를 뒤집는다.
examples: What experience teaches, it teaches slowly; and that slowness, far from being a weakness, is precisely the source of its lasting depth.
What silence communicates, it communicates indirectly; and that indirection, far from being a failure, is precisely the source of its expressive power.

drillQuestions.question: "What fiction knows, it knows differently" emphasizes fiction's _____ way of knowing.
"Far from being" means the _____ of.
"Irreplaceable" means it _____ be substituted.
This sentence functions as an _____ summary of the essay's thesis.

sentences.notes: 에세이의 핵심 역설을 격언적으로 응축한 문장. "What fiction knows, it knows differently"의 목적어 전치가 강렬한 리듬을 만들고, "far from being a limitation"이 차이를 강점으로 재정의한다. 전체 에세이의 모토 역할을 한다.

koreanAlignment.en: What fiction knows, it knows differently
koreanAlignment.ko: 소설이 아는 것을, 소설은 다르게 안다

koreanAlignment.en: and that difference, far from being a limitation
koreanAlignment.ko: 그리고 그 차이는 한계이기는커녕

koreanAlignment.en: is precisely the source of its irreplaceable value
koreanAlignment.ko: 정확히 그것의 대체 불가능한 가치의 원천이다

pronunciationNotes: irreplaceable: /ˌɪrɪˈpleɪsəbəl/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "이리플레이서블"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: If we are to cultivate a genuinely comprehensive epistemology — one adequate to the full range of human experience — then literature must be granted not merely a seat at the table but a voice in the conversation about what it means to understand.
sentences.translation: 만약 우리가 진정으로 포괄적인 인식론 — 인간 경험의 전체 범위에 적절한 것 — 을 배양하고자 한다면, 문학에는 단순히 테이블의 자리가 아니라 이해한다는 것이 무엇을 의미하는지에 대한 대화에서의 목소리가 부여되어야 한다.

grammarElements.text: If we are to cultivate a genuinely comprehensive epistemology
grammarElements.label: 조건절 (be to 구문)

grammarElements.text: one adequate to the full range of human experience
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 동격구

grammarElements.text: then literature must be granted
grammarElements.label: 주절 (수동태 + must)

grammarElements.text: not merely a seat at the table but a voice in the conversation about what it means to understand
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (not merely A but B + what절)

vocabulary.example: A comprehensive epistemology must include literary ways of knowing.
We must cultivate an epistemology adequate to human experience.

expressions.usage: "be to" 구문으로 의도된 목표를 격식적으로 표현하는 조건절

expressions.phrase: not merely a seat at the table but a voice in the conversation
expressions.meaning: 단순히 테이블의 자리가 아니라 대화에서의 목소리
expressions.usage: 참여와 발언권의 차이를 비유적으로 표현하는 정치적·학술적 관용구

patterns.pattern: If we are to [cultivate X] — [appositive] — then [Y] must be granted not merely [a seat] but [a voice in the conversation about Z]
patterns.explanation: "If ... then" 조건 구조와 "not merely A but B" 비교를 결합하여 행동 촉구(call to action)를 선언하는 마무리 패턴.
examples: If we are to build a truly inclusive democracy — one responsive to the needs of all citizens — then minority perspectives must be granted not merely representation but genuine influence in the decisions that shape policy.

If we are to develop a genuinely holistic education — one adequate to the complexity of modern life — then the arts must be granted not merely a place in the curriculum but a role in defining what competence means.

drillQuestions.question: "Epistemology" is the philosophical study of _____.
"A seat at the table" means basic _____.
"A voice in the conversation" means the right to _____.
This sentence functions as a _____ to action.

sentences.notes: 에세이의 마무리 호소(call to action). "If we are to ... then" 조건 구조가 문학의 인식론적 포함을 미래적 과제로 제시한다. "not merely a seat at the table but a voice"의 비유가 참여(seat)와 발언권(voice)의 질적 차이를 표현하며, "what it means to understand"가 인식론적 질문으로 에세이를 마무리한다.

koreanAlignment.en: If we are to cultivate a genuinely comprehensive epistemology
koreanAlignment.ko: 만약 우리가 진정으로 포괄적인 인식론을 배양하고자 한다면

koreanAlignment.en: one adequate to the full range of human experience
koreanAlignment.ko: 인간 경험의 전체 범위에 적절한 것

koreanAlignment.en: then literature must be granted not merely a seat at the table
koreanAlignment.ko: 문학에는 단순히 테이블의 자리가 아니라

koreanAlignment.en: but a voice in the conversation about what it means to understand
koreanAlignment.ko: 이해한다는 것이 무엇을 의미하는지에 대한 대화에서의 목소리가 부여되어야 한다

pronunciationNotes: epistemology: /ɪˌpɪstəˈmɑːlədʒi/ — 네 번째 음절에 강세. "이피스테몰러지"에 가깝다.
cultivate: /ˈkʌltɪveɪt/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "컬티베이트"에 가깝다.
